title: Flutter Beginner Role Mock Test
description: Evaluate fundamental Flutter knowledge for beginners covering widgets, layout, state, and basic development workflow.

options: A UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications from a single codebase
questions.explanation: Flutter is Google's open-source UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications for mobile, web, and desktop from a single codebase.

questions.text: Which programming language is used to write Flutter apps?
questions.explanation: Flutter uses Dart, a language also developed by Google, optimized for building user interfaces.
In Flutter, the UI is built entirely using widgets. Everything from a button to padding is a widget.

options: Combines common painting, positioning, and sizing widgets
questions.explanation: A Container is a convenience widget that combines padding, margin, decoration, alignment, and size constraints.

questions.text: Which widget arranges its children vertically?
questions.explanation: Column arranges children in a vertical line. Row arranges horizontally, Stack overlaps them.

questions.text: What widget displays text in Flutter?
questions.explanation: The Text widget is used to display a string of text with optional styling.
hints: Accepts a String as first argument

questions.text: What is hot reload in Flutter?
options: Quickly applying code changes without losing app state
Reinstalling the app on the device
questions.explanation: Hot reload injects updated source code into the running Dart Virtual Machine while preserving the app's state.
hints: Keeps your place in the app

questions.text: What is the entry point of a Flutter app?
questions.explanation: Every Flutter app starts execution from the `main()` function. Inside it, `runApp()` is called to attach the root widget.
hints: Same as in Dart console apps

options: It creates a new State object
It notifies the framework that the internal state has changed and the widget should rebuild
It changes the widget from Stateless to Stateful
questions.explanation: Calling `setState()` marks the widget as dirty and schedules a rebuild of the UI to reflect the new state.

questions.text: What is the difference between `StatelessWidget` and `StatefulWidget`?
options: StatelessWidget can change over time; StatefulWidget cannot
StatefulWidget can change over time; StatelessWidget is immutable
questions.explanation: StatelessWidget is immutable and its properties cannot change after build. StatefulWidget maintains mutable state that can trigger rebuilds.
